speaker: Elijah
quote_text: Is it because there is no God in Israel?
prompt_context: When Ahaziah sent messengers to inquire of Baal-zebub, the prophet intercepted them with a rebuke.
question: Who asked Ahaziah's messengers whether there was no God in Israel to inquire of?

speaker: Captain of Fifty
quote_text: O man of God, the king says, Come down
prompt_context: Ahaziah sent a military officer with fifty men to summon Elijah from the hill where he was sitting.
question: Who addressed Elijah as man of God and ordered him to come down at the king's command?

speaker: Elijah
quote_text: If I am a man of God
prompt_context: In response to the captain's demand, Elijah called down a sign proving the authority of his prophetic office.
question: Who answered the first captain by saying, "If I am a man of God"?

speaker: Second Captain of Fifty
quote_text: O man of God, this is the king's order
prompt_context: After the first group was consumed, a second officer came with fifty more and repeated the summons more urgently.
question: Who told Elijah that the king's order was for him to come down quickly?

speaker: Third Captain of Fifty
quote_text: Let my life be precious in your sight
prompt_context: Unlike the earlier captains, the third officer approached Elijah humbly and pleaded for mercy.
question: Who begged Elijah to let his life and the lives of his fifty men be precious in his sight?

speaker: Angel of the Lord
quote_text: Go down with him; do not be afraid
prompt_context: After the third captain pleaded for mercy, heavenly reassurance came to Elijah about going with the men.
question: Who told Elijah to go down with the third captain and not be afraid?

speaker: Elijah
quote_text: You shall surely die
prompt_context: Standing before Ahaziah after the inquiry to Baal-zebub, Elijah delivered the king's sentence again.
question: Who told Ahaziah that he would surely die because he had inquired of Baal-zebub?

speaker: Elijah
quote_text: Stay here
prompt_context: As Elijah prepared for his departure, he repeatedly urged Elisha not to continue following him from one stop to the next.
question: Who kept telling his companion to stay behind as they traveled before his departure?
option_c: One of the Sons of the Prophets

speaker: Sons of the Prophets
quote_text: Do you know that today the Lord will take your master?
prompt_context: At Bethel and again at Jericho, members of the prophetic community spoke quietly to Elisha about what was about to happen.
question: Who asked Elisha whether he knew the Lord would take his master from him that day?

speaker: Elisha
quote_text: Please let there be a double portion
prompt_context: Just before Elijah was taken away, Elisha was asked what he wanted before they were parted.
question: Who asked for a double portion of his master's spirit?
option_c: One of the Sons of the Prophets

speaker: Elisha
quote_text: Where is the Lord, the God of Elijah?
prompt_context: After Elijah was taken up, Elisha struck the Jordan with the fallen cloak to see whether the same power remained with him.
question: Who cried out, "Where is the Lord, the God of Elijah?" before the Jordan parted?

speaker: Men of Jericho
quote_text: The situation of this city is pleasant
prompt_context: After Elisha's return, the men of Jericho described the city's good location but bad water and unfruitful land.
question: Who told Elisha that Jericho's water was bad and the land unfruitful?

speaker: Widow
quote_text: Your servant my husband is dead
prompt_context: A desperate woman appealed to Elisha because a creditor was coming to take her two children as slaves.
question: Who told Elisha that her husband had died and the creditor was coming for her sons?

speaker: Elisha
quote_text: What shall I do for you?
prompt_context: When the widow cried out for help, Elisha first asked what resources remained in her house.
question: Who asked the widow what should be done for her and what she had in the house?

speaker: Shunammite Woman
quote_text: Let us make a small room
prompt_context: Recognizing Elisha as a holy man of God, a hospitable woman suggested a dedicated room for him in her house.
question: Who proposed making a small furnished room for Elisha on the roof?

speaker: Gehazi
quote_text: She has no son
prompt_context: When Elisha wanted to repay the Shunammite woman's kindness, his servant supplied the key detail about her household.
question: Who told Elisha that the Shunammite woman had no son and that her husband was old?

speaker: Elisha
quote_text: At this season, you shall embrace a son
prompt_context: In reward for her kindness, Elisha made an unexpected promise to the Shunammite woman.
question: Who told the Shunammite woman that she would embrace a son at that time next year?

speaker: Shunammite Woman
quote_text: Did I ask my lord for a son?
prompt_context: After her son died, the grieving mother confronted Elisha with the pain of the promise she had received.
question: Who asked Elisha whether she had asked him for a son and told him not to deceive her?

speaker: Elisha
quote_text: Gird up your loins
prompt_context: When the Shunammite woman came in distress, Elisha sent Gehazi ahead with instructions and his staff.
question: Who told Gehazi to run ahead and lay his staff on the child's face?

speaker: Sons of the Prophets
quote_text: O man of God, there is death in the pot
prompt_context: After poisonous gourds were cooked into the stew, the prophetic community cried out in alarm to Elisha.
question: Who cried out to Elisha that there was death in the pot?

speaker: Naaman
quote_text: I thought that he would surely come out
prompt_context: Offended by the prophet's simple instructions, the Syrian commander reacted angrily before being persuaded otherwise.
question: Who complained that he expected the prophet to come out and wave his hand over the place?

speaker: Naaman's Servants
quote_text: If the prophet had commanded you to do some great thing
prompt_context: When Naaman was angry at the simple command to wash, his servants reasoned with him wisely.
question: Who persuaded Naaman by asking whether he would have done some great thing if commanded?

speaker: Naaman
quote_text: Now I know
prompt_context: After obeying and being healed, Naaman returned to Elisha with confession and gratitude.
question: Who confessed that there was no God in all the earth but in Israel after being healed?

speaker: Naaman
quote_text: Please let there be given to your servant
prompt_context: After his healing, Naaman asked for soil from Israel and sought pardon for a future act connected to his royal duties.
question: Who asked Elisha for two mule-loads of earth and pardon when bowing in the house of Rimmon?

speaker: Gehazi
quote_text: My master has spared this Naaman
prompt_context: After Elisha refused Naaman's gifts, Gehazi decided to pursue the Syrian secretly for personal gain.
question: Who said that his master had spared Naaman too much by not accepting from him what he brought?

speaker: Gehazi
quote_text: My master has sent me
prompt_context: When Naaman saw him running after the chariot, Gehazi invented a story about visitors needing money and garments.
question: Who lied to Naaman by saying that Elisha had sent him for silver and garments?
option_c: One of the Sons of the Prophets

speaker: Elisha
quote_text: Did not my heart go when the man turned?
prompt_context: After Gehazi hid the gifts and stood before Elisha, the prophet exposed both the lie and the greed behind it.
question: Who asked Gehazi whether his heart had not gone with Naaman when he turned from the chariot?

speaker: Sons of the Prophets
quote_text: The place where we dwell is too small
prompt_context: As the prophetic community grew, they asked Elisha for permission to build a larger dwelling near the Jordan.
question: Who told Elisha that the place where they lived under him was too small?

speaker: One of the Sons of the Prophets
quote_text: Alas, my master
prompt_context: While felling logs by the Jordan, a borrowed iron axe head fell into the water, and one of the men cried out.
question: Who cried to Elisha when the borrowed axe head fell into the water?
option_b: One of the Sons of the Prophets
correct_answer: One of the Sons of the Prophets

speaker: King of Aram
quote_text: At such and such a place shall be my camp
prompt_context: While planning raids against Israel, the Aramean king repeatedly gave military directions that were somehow exposed.
question: Who kept telling his servants where his camp would be set during the war with Israel?

speaker: Elisha's Servant
quote_text: Alas, my master! What shall we do?
prompt_context: When the city was surrounded by horses and chariots, Elisha's attendant panicked at the sight.
question: Who asked Elisha what they should do when the Aramean army surrounded the city?
option_d: One of the Sons of the Prophets

speaker: Elisha
quote_text: Do not be afraid
prompt_context: In response to his servant's fear, Elisha assured him that the unseen divine force was greater than the visible enemy host.
question: Who told his servant not to be afraid because those with them were more than those with the enemy?

speaker: King of Israel
quote_text: My father, shall I strike them down?
prompt_context: After the blinded Arameans were led into Samaria, the king of Israel asked Elisha how to handle them.
question: Who asked Elisha twice whether he should strike down the captured Arameans?

speaker: Elisha
quote_text: Tomorrow about this time
prompt_context: During the severe siege of Samaria, Elisha announced an astonishing reversal in food prices by the next day.
question: Who prophesied that flour and barley would be sold cheaply in Samaria by the next day?

speaker: Royal Officer
quote_text: If the Lord himself should make windows in heaven
prompt_context: Hearing Elisha's prophecy about sudden abundance, an officer assisting the king answered with unbelief.
question: Who said that even if the Lord made windows in heaven, such a thing could not happen?

speaker: Four Lepers
quote_text: Why are we sitting here until we die?
prompt_context: Outside besieged Samaria, four desperate men reasoned together about entering the Aramean camp.
question: Who asked why they should sit where they were until they died during the siege?

speaker: Four Lepers
quote_text: We are not doing right
prompt_context: After discovering the abandoned Aramean camp and first hoarding the spoils, the men reconsidered and decided to report the good news.
question: Who said they were not doing right by keeping silent on a day of good news?

speaker: Elisha
quote_text: Arise and depart
prompt_context: Before a coming famine, Elisha warned the Shunammite woman to live elsewhere for a time.
question: Who told the Shunammite woman to arise and sojourn wherever she could because a famine was coming?

speaker: Elisha
quote_text: You shall certainly recover, but the Lord has shown me he shall certainly die
prompt_context: When Hazael came with gifts to inquire about Ben-hadad's illness, Elisha gave a layered answer about the king's future.
question: Who told Hazael that Ben-hadad would recover from the illness, yet would certainly die?

speaker: Elisha
quote_text: The Lord has shown me that you are to be king
prompt_context: After weeping over the future harm Hazael would do to Israel, Elisha revealed what Hazael was to become.
question: Who told Hazael that the Lord had shown him he would be king over Aram?

speaker: Young Prophet
quote_text: Thus says the Lord
prompt_context: Sent by Elisha, a young prophet secretly anointed Jehu and delivered God's judgment on Ahab's house.
question: Who anointed Jehu and declared the Lord's judgment against the house of Ahab?

speaker: Jehu
quote_text: What do you have to do with peace?
prompt_context: As messengers rode out asking whether all was well, Jehu gave a sharp reply and told them to fall in behind him.
question: Who kept answering the mounted messengers, "What do you have to do with peace?"

speaker: Joram
quote_text: Is it peace, Jehu?
prompt_context: When the king of Israel finally met Jehu in person, he asked the question the messengers had already raised.
question: Who asked Jehu, "Is it peace?" before being answered with a reference to Jezebel's whorings and sorceries?

speaker: Jehu
quote_text: Who is on my side?
prompt_context: After arriving at Jezreel, Jehu looked up and called out to those in the palace before Jezebel's death.
question: Who cried out, "Who is on my side? Who?" before Jezebel was thrown down?

speaker: Athaliah
quote_text: Treason! Treason!
prompt_context: When the hidden young king was revealed in the temple, Athaliah reacted in horror at the coup against her rule.
question: Who cried, "Treason! Treason!" when Joash was presented as king?

speaker: Jehoiada
quote_text: Bring her out between the ranks
prompt_context: After Athaliah's cry in the temple, the priest gave precise instructions for handling her without defiling the holy place.
question: Who ordered that Athaliah be brought out and killed outside the temple precincts?

speaker: Rabshakeh
quote_text: What is this trust?
prompt_context: When Assyria threatened Judah, Hezekiah's officials met the Assyrian spokesman, who mocked Judah's confidence.
question: Who asked Judah, "What is this trust that you trust in?" during the Assyrian confrontation?

speaker: Hezekiah's Officials
quote_text: Please speak in Aramaic
prompt_context: During the Assyrian confrontation, Hezekiah's officials tried to keep the people on the wall from understanding the threatening speech.
question: Who asked Rabshakeh to speak in Aramaic instead of the language of Judah?

speaker: Rabshakeh
quote_text: Do not let Hezekiah deceive you
prompt_context: Ignoring the officials' request, the Assyrian spokesman raised his voice to the people on the wall with propaganda.
question: Who warned the people not to let Hezekiah deceive them or make them trust in the Lord?

speaker: Hezekiah's Servants
quote_text: A day of distress
prompt_context: After Rabshakeh's threats, Hezekiah sent officials to Isaiah to describe Jerusalem's desperate situation.
question: Who described to Isaiah the crisis as a day of distress, rebuke, and disgrace?

speaker: Hezekiah's Servants
quote_text: This day is a day of distress
prompt_context: Sent to Isaiah, the king's representatives described the desperate crisis facing Jerusalem.
question: Who told Isaiah that the day was one of distress, rebuke, and disgrace?

speaker: Isaiah
quote_text: Do not be afraid
prompt_context: In response to Hezekiah's appeal, Isaiah sent a message assuring the king about the fate of the Assyrian threat.
question: Who told Hezekiah's servants not to be afraid of the words they had heard from Assyria?

speaker: Hezekiah
quote_text: Save us, please
prompt_context: After receiving Sennacherib's blasphemous message, Hezekiah spread the letter before the Lord and prayed for deliverance.
question: Who prayed that the Lord would save Jerusalem so all kingdoms might know that he alone is God?

speaker: Isaiah
quote_text: Thus says the Lord
prompt_context: After Hezekiah's prayer, Isaiah sent back the Lord's answer regarding Sennacherib and Jerusalem.
question: Who sent Hezekiah the message beginning, "Thus says the Lord" in answer to his prayer?

speaker: Isaiah
quote_text: Set your house in order
prompt_context: When Hezekiah became sick unto death, the prophet brought him a sobering message.
question: Who told Hezekiah to set his house in order because he would die and not recover?

speaker: God
quote_text: I have heard your prayer
prompt_context: After Hezekiah wept and prayed, the Lord sent word back through Isaiah with healing and added years.
question: Who said to Hezekiah, through Isaiah, "I have heard your prayer"?

speaker: Josiah
quote_text: Go, inquire of the Lord for me
prompt_context: After hearing the Book of the Law read aloud, the king realized how great Judah's guilt was and sought prophetic guidance.
question: Who ordered that inquiry be made of the Lord after hearing the Book of the Law?

speaker: Huldah
quote_text: Thus says the Lord
prompt_context: When Josiah's delegation came to the prophetess, she announced coming judgment because Judah had forsaken the Lord.
question: Who declared to Josiah's messengers that disaster would come on that place because of its people's unfaithfulness?

speaker: Huldah
quote_text: Because your heart was penitent
prompt_context: Though judgment on Judah was certain, the prophetess gave Josiah a personal word of mercy because he had humbled himself.
question: Who told Josiah that he would be gathered to his grave in peace because his heart was penitent?

speaker: Gedaliah
quote_text: Do not be afraid of the servants of the Chaldeans
prompt_context: After Jerusalem's fall, the Babylonian-appointed governor urged the remaining people to settle peacefully in the land.
question: Who told the remnant in Judah not to be afraid of serving the Chaldeans?
